data.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL]Re: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

body.content:
Hi Erik – Confirming the form was received. Our operations team will take it from here to work with you to coordinate the offboarding during the AWS Transfer period window for April billing. Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you, RM

 

Rick Marshbanks

AWS Business Development Executive

St Petersburg, FL

(248) 622.0869

 

 

From: Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>
Sent: Thursday, March 19, 2026 4:41 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>
Subject: [EXTERNAL]Re: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

 

Hi Rick,

 

It has been awhile.  I just wanted to advise you that I put in the AWS Outbound Transfer Request for the selected accounts that we are transitioning.  Please advise if there is anything missing with the provided information on the form.

 

The other note, which Matt asked earlier, is the confirmation of the 8% on the accounts that will remain with Ingram Micro.  I know you only represent the US Geography, I do see Lalita on the email thread, maybe she can oversee that gets passed along as of April 1, 2026.

 

Thank you

 

Erik

 

Erik Tanguay

Project Control Officer

+1 (514) 673-7552
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 9:51 AM
To: Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

 

Hi Rick,

Thanks for your collaboration here, again, we’re only porting US customers, but will remain with Ingram for the UK and Canadian regions.

 

On that note, can you confirm steps to ensure we’ll receive the 8% discount in those geographies effective 4/1?

 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com>
Sent: February 18, 2026 5:33 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <marc.pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <adam.nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

 

+@Chandrashekar, Subramanya / @Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita

 

Hi Matt, Thank you for the note and for the partnership we have built together. While I am disappointed to hear we will not be with you for the next phase of your AWS Partner Journey, I sincerely appreciate the collaboration and trust Aptum has placed in Ingram Micro throughout our relationship. Given the April 1 transition timeline, I welcome the opportunity to schedule a brief call with the broader team to review the offboarding process to help ensure everything runs smoothly. This would also allow us to align on any country-specific considerations and required documentation so there are no disruptions.

 

I would also value the opportunity to better understand the factors that led up to this decision. If there are areas where we could have delivered more value, I want to ensure we take that feedback constructively as we continue to improve and adapt as a global business unit.  If you prefer, I am happy to connect one-on-one as well. Please let me know what works best for you and your team.

 

Regardless of the path forward, we remain committed to supporting Aptum through a seamless transition and keeping the door open for future collaboration.

 

Respectfully, RM

 

Rick Marshbanks

AWS Business Development Executive

St Petersburg, FL

(248) 622.0869

 

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 4:03 PM
To: AWS Cloud Operations <awscloudops@ingrammicro.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: [EXTERNAL]Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

 

Dear Ingram Micro Team,

Please accept this message as notice that we intend to transition our AWS DSA to another aggregator effective April 1.

 

We appreciate the support and partnership we have with Ingram Micro and thank you for your assistance throughout our relationship.

 

We will circle back closer to April with regards to next steps and documentation needed to complete this transition.

Thanks,

 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

data.subject: 🚧 Vos infrastructures vieillissent et l'inaction n'est pas une option!

body.content:
You don't often get email from evenementslesaffaires@groupecontex.ca. Learn why this is important
Si vous avez des difficultés à lire ce courriel cliquez ici
Profitez de 25 % de rabais sur le prix régulier. Code : RV25 jusqu’au 27 mars 2026 inclus!
Municipalités? 10 % de rabais supplémentaire (contactez : evenementslesaffaires@groupecontex.ca)
Bonjour Adam,  

Sous nos pieds, un réseau invisible tient nos villes en vie. Aqueducs centenaires, tunnels de métro qui s'effritent, voirie qui cède sous le poids des hivers : les infrastructures municipales québécoises sont à la croisée des chemins. Les besoins dépassent les capacités, les budgets sont contraints, et attendre n'est plus une option.

Des solutions existent pourtant. Le 22 avril prochain, notre conférence Réinventer la ville : infrastructures réunira décideur·euses, ingénieur·es et expert·es pour partager des approches concrètes : financer autrement, prioriser intelligemment, moderniser sans tout recommencer.

Une journée dense, tournée vers l'action, avec des cas réels venus de Montréal, du Québec et même de Belgique.

Parce que rebâtir nos villes commence par savoir exactement où elles en sont.
👉 Je m'inscris!
 
 
PLUS QU'UNE SEMAINE POUR RÉSERVER VOTRE PLACE À PRIX RÉDUIT!

Profitez de 25 % de rabais sur le prix régulier avec le code promotionnel RV25 jusqu’au 27 mars 2026 inclus!

Vous êtes une municipalité? Profitez de 10 % de rabais supplémentaire!
Contactez notre service à la clientèle : evenementslesaffaires@groupecontex.ca
Je profite du rabais de 25%
 
🌟 Des voix qui façonnent la ville de demain 🌟

Des leaders influent·es et des expert·es engagé·es viendront partager leur vision des grands enjeux urbains. À travers des innovations techniques et des stratégies concrètes, ils et elles mettront en lumière comment mieux planifier, réaliser et financer des infrastructures municipales durables et performantes, tout en misant sur une collaboration étroite entre acteurs publics et partenaires privés.
🎤 Des contenus ancrés dans la réalité 
Au programme :

  • Une analyse stratégique des infrastructures urbaines
  • Des panels d’expert·es sur les innovations techniques et financières
  • Des études de cas concrètes (métro de Montréal et réseaux thermiques)
  • Des échanges pour repenser la collaboration entre acteurs publics et privés
Et bien plus encore!

Chaque intervention est pensée pour vous permettre de repartir avec des idées applicables, adaptées aux réalités du terrain.

👉 Je découvre la programmation!

Hi Matthew,
Great to meet you virtually, and thank you for your patience while I was out of the office.
Could you please share the current status or confirmation from AWS regarding your DSA transfer request? The DSA transfer process can take up to three weeks, so we are probably looking at an April start date for partnership and transferring accounts, as we must have your DSA in place before completing the CTA account(s) transfer.
Additionally, we will need to get Aptum set up as a US TD SYNNEX partner with an account number and Net 30 credit terms. Please complete our TD SYNNEX Credit Application here: Become a Customer - TD SYNNEX USA and let me know if you have any questions.
I’m available for a call today if you’d like to walk through the process together.
Thank you,

Ciara Perkins

Multi-Cloud Business Development Representative III

(480) 936-5447


Reserve Time on My Calendar


From: Thomson, Christine <Christine.Thomson@tdsynnex.com>
Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 5:57 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Sankar, Rupesh <Rupesh.Sankar@tdsynnex.com>; Roodi, Maz <maz.roodi@tdsynnex.com>; Tammo, Gina <Gina.Tammo@tdsynnex.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Perkins, Ciara <ciara.perkins@tdsynnex.com>; Neumeier, Debra <debra.neumeier@tdsynnex.com>
Subject: APTUM/AWS CTA transfer
 

+@Perkins, Ciara @Neumeier, Debra

 

Hi Matthew,

 

This is fantastic news~ Thank you for choosing to move forward with TD SYNNEX and for giving us the opportunity to earn your business. We are genuinely excited to support you & your team and ensure a smooth transition.

 

Rupesh, Maz, and I will remain fully engaged and dedicated to supporting all your needs for the Canadian region. In the US, Ciara Perkins (Business Development Rep, AWS)  and Deb Neumeier (Sr. Sales Manager) will be your go-to contacts, and they're ready to provide the white glove service Aptum needs to be fully onboarded. Ciara and Deb, I'm looping you in here so you can assist Matthew and the team in getting set up and operational in the US (CTA is requested for March 22). Your support will be invaluable in ensuring a seamless experience for everyone involved.  Rupesh will send a separate email with the background information to get you both fully up to speed on this opportunity.

 

In the meantime, I suggest we push ahead in Canada in getting the Aptum account activated and ready for future Canadian business. This way, you'll have everything in place and can hit the ground running as you expand.

 

Thanks again, Matthew—we truly appreciate the partnership and look forward to continuing a successful journey together.

 

 

Christine Thomson

Meeting Summary

 

AWS Partner Program Changes (Effective April 1st)

The meeting focused on major AWS partner program changes being rolled out, with Gina (TD SYNNEX subject matter expert) presenting a comprehensive overview. Key changes include a new base discount structure, deprecation of several legacy incentives, and new operational requirements. The changes represent a significant overhaul of how AWS incentivizes channel partners, with many updates piling up simultaneously affecting TD SYNNEX's ~4,900 global partners.

Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect
- April 1st: New base benefit, deprecation of technology component incentive, new NCI, deprecation of POD, PSD, and CEI incentives

 

Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)

The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.

Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)
- Existing Accounts (formerly "Shareshift"): Only 2% discount applies to any member account spending >$50,000/month prior to linking
- Existing Account Policy: Currently permanent (no time limit); TD SYNNEX has lobbied AWS to reduce to 1–2 years but AWS has not agreed
- First billing reflection: May bill (for April usage) will be the first to reflect the new 8% discount
- Competency benefits: Financial discount for competency is now absorbed into base rate, but competencies still provide lead generation and increased MDF access

 

Existing Accounts / Shareshift Classification

The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.

Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount
- Accounts at $50,000+ receive only 2% (existing account rate)
- Exception process available (business use case submission), but Gina noted ~80% failure rate
- Acceptable exception reasons: prior reseller went out of business, unique services the new reseller provides that the old one cannot
- Workarounds discussed:
- Modernization into new member accounts (acceptable)
- Pure lift-and-shift to new member account (frowned upon)
- Normalizing spend across member accounts before linking
- Specific Optum fintech customer noted: Has been consuming direct; team to explore business case exception with AWS to classify as base account given Optum's involvement since inception

 

DSA Migration & Timing (Moving to TD SYNNEX)

Optum is planning to move their US legal entity first, with Canada and UK as a secondary phase. The migration process and timing requirements were clarified.

Status Details:
- Process: Submit APN ID + DSA move request by region to AWS; Rupesh to provide email address
- Deadline: DSA move and CTA must be completed before end-user reporting closes (last Friday of the month — approximately March 22–23rd for April 1st effective date)
- Consent to Assign (CTA): TD SYNNEX will prepare a consolidated document with all payer IDs; Optum signs once; then a 10–15 minute call with both distributors' operations teams to transfer root access
- Important: If DSA is not moved in time, neither old nor new distributor receives the discount (street price applies)
- Canada: Rupesh remains account manager for both US and Canada regions; billing currency can be USD or CAD based on preference
- Former aggregator behavior: May attempt to retain the partner; TD SYNNEX noted competitors have dragged their feet in the past

 

New Customer Incentive (NCI)

NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.

Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking
- Benefit: Extra 4% discount for 3 years (vs. POD's 5% for 2 years)
- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root

 

Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection

Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.

Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases
- TD SYNNEX noted as better positioned to absorb bad debt than individual partners

 

MDF (Market Development Funds)

TD SYNNEX offers an MDF match program tied to AWS MDF allocations.

Status Details:
- AWS MDF: Advanced partners with competency can access up to $50,000/year (typically ~$25,000); AWS covers 50% after proof of performance
- TD SYNNEX Match: Matches what AWS contributes (covering the other 50%)
- Allocation: Annual basis, not quarterly/monthly; based on qualification level and partner activity
- One-off events: Partners can request ad hoc MDF participation with a business case (ROI, cost, timing)
- TD SYNNEX distributes limited AWS funding across ~1,300–1,400 North America partners; more active partners get more access

 

Payment Terms

Payment terms are determined individually based on credit rating and financials.

Status Details:
- Process: TD SYNNEX credit team reviews financials during onboarding to establish credit line and terms
- Typical terms: Net 30 standard; Net 45 likely achievable; Net 90 rare (requires ~$2–4M/month in AWS spend)
- Terms cannot be confirmed in advance — financial review required first
- Christine to follow up offline on credit/payment term specifics

  • DSA Migration Scope: US legal entity first; Canada and UK as secondary phase
  • Billing Currency: US dollars preferred for current migration

  • TD SYNNEX Team Structure: Rupesh = primary account manager (North America); Gina = programs SME (AWS incentives, PPA, partner-led support); Christine = escalation; Maz = supervisor/escalation; pre-sales technical resource also assigned

  • The technical competency discount (previously 4%) has been absorbed into the new 8% base rate — no additional financial discount for holding a competency, though other benefits (leads, MDF access) remain
  • SPP certification no longer provides an incremental financial discount under the new structure
  • Billing Transfer is a strategic tool to reduce customer friction around root access and simplifies operational onboarding
  • All program changes represent a significant simultaneous shift — partners are encouraged to act quickly ahead of April 1st to avoid billing disruptions

Hi Adam,

Last year, you were tracking Technology Services trends. So here’s what you need to know now: AI Economics™ is dismantling seat-based SaaS growth—and forcing a shift to outcome delivery where services become the profit engine.

The State of Technology Services 2026 breaks down what’s actually changing—and what AI-native leaders are doing differently. It’s not opinion-based. It’s TSIA research mapping how the market is splitting right now.

 
Inside, you'll see:
  • Why services, not software or tech alone, will define the AI-era winners.
  • What’s actually blocking scale in the last mile (data, integration, governance, and talent).

AI isn’t eliminating technology services. It’s making them indispensible—because outcomes don’t deliver themselves.

Best,

New Customer Incentive (NCI)

NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.

Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking

- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root

 

Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection

Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.

Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases

MDF (Market Development Funds)

Status Details:

Payment Terms

Payment terms are determined individually based on credit rating and financials.

Status Details:

Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect

Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)

The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.

Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)

The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.

Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount

Subject: RE: Placeholder Event: Preview of the new program through TD SYNNEX along with all the new incentives

 

Hi Matthew,

 

Please find the minutes from today’s call below.

 

***These are AI-generated minutes based on the discussion. Please review for accuracy.

 

Meeting Summary

 

AWS Partner Program Changes (Effective April 1st)

The meeting focused on major AWS partner program changes being rolled out, with Gina (TD SYNNEX subject matter expert) presenting a comprehensive overview. Key changes include a new base discount structure, deprecation of several legacy incentives, and new operational requirements. The changes represent a significant overhaul of how AWS incentivizes channel partners, with many updates piling up simultaneously affecting TD SYNNEX's ~4,900 global partners.

Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect
- April 1st: New base benefit, deprecation of technology component incentive, new NCI, deprecation of POD, PSD, and CEI incentives

 

Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)

The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.

Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)
- Existing Accounts (formerly "Shareshift"): Only 2% discount applies to any member account spending >$50,000/month prior to linking
- Existing Account Policy: Currently permanent (no time limit); TD SYNNEX has lobbied AWS to reduce to 1–2 years but AWS has not agreed
- First billing reflection: May bill (for April usage) will be the first to reflect the new 8% discount
- Competency benefits: Financial discount for competency is now absorbed into base rate, but competencies still provide lead generation and increased MDF access

 

Existing Accounts / Shareshift Classification

The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.

Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount
- Accounts at $50,000+ receive only 2% (existing account rate)
- Exception process available (business use case submission), but Gina noted ~80% failure rate
- Acceptable exception reasons: prior reseller went out of business, unique services the new reseller provides that the old one cannot
- Workarounds discussed:
- Modernization into new member accounts (acceptable)
- Pure lift-and-shift to new member account (frowned upon)
- Normalizing spend across member accounts before linking
- Specific Optum fintech customer noted: Has been consuming direct; team to explore business case exception with AWS to classify as base account given Optum's involvement since inception

 

DSA Migration & Timing (Moving to TD SYNNEX)

Optum is planning to move their US legal entity first, with Canada and UK as a secondary phase. The migration process and timing requirements were clarified.

Status Details:
- Process: Submit APN ID + DSA move request by region to AWS; Rupesh to provide email address
- Deadline: DSA move and CTA must be completed before end-user reporting closes (last Friday of the month — approximately March 22–23rd for April 1st effective date)
- Consent to Assign (CTA): TD SYNNEX will prepare a consolidated document with all payer IDs; Optum signs once; then a 10–15 minute call with both distributors' operations teams to transfer root access
- Important: If DSA is not moved in time, neither old nor new distributor receives the discount (street price applies)
- Canada: Rupesh remains account manager for both US and Canada regions; billing currency can be USD or CAD based on preference
- Former aggregator behavior: May attempt to retain the partner; TD SYNNEX noted competitors have dragged their feet in the past

 

New Customer Incentive (NCI)

NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.

Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking
- Benefit: Extra 4% discount for 3 years (vs. POD's 5% for 2 years)
- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root

 

Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection

Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.

Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases
- TD SYNNEX noted as better positioned to absorb bad debt than individual partners

 

MDF (Market Development Funds)

TD SYNNEX offers an MDF match program tied to AWS MDF allocations.

Status Details:
- AWS MDF: Advanced partners with competency can access up to $50,000/year (typically ~$25,000); AWS covers 50% after proof of performance
- TD SYNNEX Match: Matches what AWS contributes (covering the other 50%)
- Allocation: Annual basis, not quarterly/monthly; based on qualification level and partner activity
- One-off events: Partners can request ad hoc MDF participation with a business case (ROI, cost, timing)
- TD SYNNEX distributes limited AWS funding across ~1,300–1,400 North America partners; more active partners get more access

 

Payment Terms

Payment terms are determined individually based on credit rating and financials.

Status Details:
- Process: TD SYNNEX credit team reviews financials during onboarding to establish credit line and terms
- Typical terms: Net 30 standard; Net 45 likely achievable; Net 90 rare (requires ~$2–4M/month in AWS spend)
- Terms cannot be confirmed in advance — financial review required first
- Christine to follow up offline on credit/payment term specifics

 

✅ Decisions Made

  • DSA Migration Scope: US legal entity first; Canada and UK as secondary phase
  • Billing Currency: US dollars preferred for current migration
  • Deck Sharing: Gina to share the comprehensive partner-facing program deck with the Optum/Matthew team

 

🎯 Action Items

@Rupesh
- Provide AWS email address for DSA move request submission
- Prepare consolidated Consent to Assign (CTA) document with all payer IDs
- Schedule operations call with former aggregator for root access transfer
- Follow up on Adam's questions submitted via chat

@Gina
- Share comprehensive partner-facing program deck (covering all PPA, NCI, support plan changes)
- Support business case exception request for Optum's fintech customer (direct-consuming client) to be classified as base account

@Christine
- Follow up offline on payment terms and credit line process for Optum
- Address Adam's questions from the chat via email

@Matthew / @Adam / @Marc (Optum Team)
- Initiate DSA move request for US region (send APN ID + request to move DSA from current distributor to TD SYNNEX)
- Complete CTA signing
- Evaluate fintech customer migration strategy (business case exception vs. modernization approach)
- Provide financials to TD SYNNEX credit team for payment terms assessment
- Review email sent by Marc on Tuesday covering payment terms and MDF details

 

📊 Updates Shared

  • TD SYNNEX Team Structure: Rupesh = primary account manager (North America); Gina = programs SME (AWS incentives, PPA, partner-led support); Christine = escalation; Maz = supervisor/escalation; pre-sales technical resource also assigned
  • Gina's availability note: High demand across many partners; Rupesh coordinates SME engagement as needed

 

📋 Additional Notes

  • The technical competency discount (previously 4%) has been absorbed into the new 8% base rate — no additional financial discount for holding a competency, though other benefits (leads, MDF access) remain
  • SPP certification no longer provides an incremental financial discount under the new structure
  • Billing Transfer is a strategic tool to reduce customer friction around root access and simplifies operational onboarding
  • All program changes represent a significant simultaneous shift — partners are encouraged to act quickly ahead of April 1st to avoid billing disruptions

 

 

Thanks & Regards

 

Rupesh Sankar

Business Development Manager

TD SYNNEX

6911 Creditview Road

Mississauga, ON, L5N 8G1

647 807-8277

From: Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>
Date: Wednesday, 18 March 2026 at 3:01 p.m.
To: Mario Zuñiga <mzuniga@smart-scale.com>
Cc: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>, Adam Wilson <adamandleticiawilson@gmail.com>
Subject: Required Licensing Update for Hosted SQL Servers

Hi Mario,
 I know this has been an ongoing discussion, and I understand your preference is to continue running SQL Server 2022 Standard on "Data Transformation Smart Scale – Siegfried" (SID 7862436) and "Smart Scale 210" (SID 7930701).
 Unfortunately, we're unable to continue hosting these servers under the current licensing arrangement due to Microsoft compliance requirements. The licenses you currently hold are Server+CAL perpetual licenses, which Microsoft does not permit for use in hosted or cloud environments. Only per-core licenses with active Software Assurance qualify for License Mobility are allowed; that's a Microsoft requirement, not an Aptum one.
 I understand this is frustrating, especially given the length of your relationship with us. But in the event of a Microsoft audit, Aptum would be held liable, and the potential exposure is significant. We need to resolve this within the next 60 days. I have been instructed by the organization that if we do not get this resolved by May 22, we will have to decommission these two servers.
 We do want to work with you to find a path forward that minimizes impact to your operations. There are options that may be more cost-effective than purchasing per-core licenses directly, for example, migrating your SQL workloads to Azure SQL, where licensing is included in the service. We'd like to walk you through these on a call.
 Would you be available for a 30-minute call this week or next to review the options and identify the most practical approach for your situation?
 Let me know what your availability looks like and we'll coordinate on our side.
 Thanks,
Elizabeth Perez
Inside Account Manager

+1 (210) 441-4686

To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Mohammadi, Maz <Mazyar.Mohammadi@tdsynnex.com>; Tammo, Gina <Gina.Tammo@tdsynnex.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Thomson, Christine <Christine.Thomson@tdsynnex.com>
Subject: RE: Placeholder Event: Preview of the new program through TD SYNNEX along with all the new incentives

 

Hi Matthew,

 

Please find the minutes from today’s call below.

 

***These are AI-generated minutes based on the discussion. Please review for accuracy.

 

Meeting Summary

 

AWS Partner Program Changes (Effective April 1st)

The meeting focused on major AWS partner program changes being rolled out, with Gina (TD SYNNEX subject matter expert) presenting a comprehensive overview. Key changes include a new base discount structure, deprecation of several legacy incentives, and new operational requirements. The changes represent a significant overhaul of how AWS incentivizes channel partners, with many updates piling up simultaneously affecting TD SYNNEX's ~4,900 global partners.

Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect
- April 1st: New base benefit, deprecation of technology component incentive, new NCI, deprecation of POD, PSD, and CEI incentives

 

Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)

The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.

Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)
- Existing Accounts (formerly "Shareshift"): Only 2% discount applies to any member account spending >$50,000/month prior to linking
- Existing Account Policy: Currently permanent (no time limit); TD SYNNEX has lobbied AWS to reduce to 1–2 years but AWS has not agreed
- First billing reflection: May bill (for April usage) will be the first to reflect the new 8% discount
- Competency benefits: Financial discount for competency is now absorbed into base rate, but competencies still provide lead generation and increased MDF access

 

Existing Accounts / Shareshift Classification

The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.

Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount
- Accounts at $50,000+ receive only 2% (existing account rate)
- Exception process available (business use case submission), but Gina noted ~80% failure rate
- Acceptable exception reasons: prior reseller went out of business, unique services the new reseller provides that the old one cannot
- Workarounds discussed:
- Modernization into new member accounts (acceptable)
- Pure lift-and-shift to new member account (frowned upon)
- Normalizing spend across member accounts before linking
- Specific Optum fintech customer noted: Has been consuming direct; team to explore business case exception with AWS to classify as base account given Optum's involvement since inception

 

DSA Migration & Timing (Moving to TD SYNNEX)

Optum is planning to move their US legal entity first, with Canada and UK as a secondary phase. The migration process and timing requirements were clarified.

Status Details:
- Process: Submit APN ID + DSA move request by region to AWS; Rupesh to provide email address
- Deadline: DSA move and CTA must be completed before end-user reporting closes (last Friday of the month — approximately March 22–23rd for April 1st effective date)
- Consent to Assign (CTA): TD SYNNEX will prepare a consolidated document with all payer IDs; Optum signs once; then a 10–15 minute call with both distributors' operations teams to transfer root access
- Important: If DSA is not moved in time, neither old nor new distributor receives the discount (street price applies)
- Canada: Rupesh remains account manager for both US and Canada regions; billing currency can be USD or CAD based on preference
- Former aggregator behavior: May attempt to retain the partner; TD SYNNEX noted competitors have dragged their feet in the past

 

New Customer Incentive (NCI)

NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.

Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking
- Benefit: Extra 4% discount for 3 years (vs. POD's 5% for 2 years)
- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root

 

Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection

Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.

Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases
- TD SYNNEX noted as better positioned to absorb bad debt than individual partners

 

MDF (Market Development Funds)

TD SYNNEX offers an MDF match program tied to AWS MDF allocations.

Status Details:
- AWS MDF: Advanced partners with competency can access up to $50,000/year (typically ~$25,000); AWS covers 50% after proof of performance
- TD SYNNEX Match: Matches what AWS contributes (covering the other 50%)
- Allocation: Annual basis, not quarterly/monthly; based on qualification level and partner activity
- One-off events: Partners can request ad hoc MDF participation with a business case (ROI, cost, timing)
- TD SYNNEX distributes limited AWS funding across ~1,300–1,400 North America partners; more active partners get more access

 

Payment Terms

Payment terms are determined individually based on credit rating and financials.

Status Details:
- Process: TD SYNNEX credit team reviews financials during onboarding to establish credit line and terms
- Typical terms: Net 30 standard; Net 45 likely achievable; Net 90 rare (requires ~$2–4M/month in AWS spend)
- Terms cannot be confirmed in advance — financial review required first
- Christine to follow up offline on credit/payment term specifics

 

✅ Decisions Made

  • DSA Migration Scope: US legal entity first; Canada and UK as secondary phase
  • Billing Currency: US dollars preferred for current migration
  • Deck Sharing: Gina to share the comprehensive partner-facing program deck with the Optum/Matthew team

 

🎯 Action Items

@Rupesh
- Provide AWS email address for DSA move request submission
- Prepare consolidated Consent to Assign (CTA) document with all payer IDs
- Schedule operations call with former aggregator for root access transfer
- Follow up on Adam's questions submitted via chat

@Gina
- Share comprehensive partner-facing program deck (covering all PPA, NCI, support plan changes)
- Support business case exception request for Optum's fintech customer (direct-consuming client) to be classified as base account

@Christine
- Follow up offline on payment terms and credit line process for Optum
- Address Adam's questions from the chat via email

@Matthew / @Adam / @Marc (Optum Team)
- Initiate DSA move request for US region (send APN ID + request to move DSA from current distributor to TD SYNNEX)
- Complete CTA signing
- Evaluate fintech customer migration strategy (business case exception vs. modernization approach)
- Provide financials to TD SYNNEX credit team for payment terms assessment
- Review email sent by Marc on Tuesday covering payment terms and MDF details

 

📊 Updates Shared

  • TD SYNNEX Team Structure: Rupesh = primary account manager (North America); Gina = programs SME (AWS incentives, PPA, partner-led support); Christine = escalation; Maz = supervisor/escalation; pre-sales technical resource also assigned
  • Gina's availability note: High demand across many partners; Rupesh coordinates SME engagement as needed

 

📋 Additional Notes

  • The technical competency discount (previously 4%) has been absorbed into the new 8% base rate — no additional financial discount for holding a competency, though other benefits (leads, MDF access) remain
  • SPP certification no longer provides an incremental financial discount under the new structure
  • Billing Transfer is a strategic tool to reduce customer friction around root access and simplifies operational onboarding
  • All program changes represent a significant simultaneous shift — partners are encouraged to act quickly ahead of April 1st to avoid billing disruptions

 

 

Thanks & Regards

 

Rupesh Sankar

Business Development Manager

TD SYNNEX

data.subject: See how your Professional Services model compares

body.content:
Assess, benchmark, and prioritize with confidence
View this email in your browser

Hi Adam,

Most Professional Services leaders feel the pressure.

Utilization targets are harder to hit.
Margins are under scrutiny.
Customers expect outcomes, not effort.

But very few can answer this with confidence: Where does our Professional Services model actually break in the AI era?

Not all delivery models respond to AI the same way.

Benchmark your organization

Some services become ROI engines. Others quietly turn into profit leaks as productivity rises and billable effort falls. The challenge is visibility.


Without clear benchmarks, many PS organizations:

  • Overinvest in labor-heavy services that no longer scale.
  • Defend utilization models that weaken as AI accelerates delivery.
  • Miss where outcome ownership should replace hours-based pricing.
This is where leading Professional Services organizations separate instinct from insight.

They use industry data to understand:

  • Which services remain resilient as productivity accelerates.
  • How their delivery model compares to peers.
  • Where to adapt before revenue gaps widen.

The leaders who diagnose early have options. The rest are left reacting.

See how TSIA Performance Optimizer helps leaders benchmark and pressure-test their Professional Services strategy, at no cost.

body.content:
You don't often get email from no-reply@amazonaws.com. Learn why this is important
Hello,


Read carefully and take action to prevent unwanted charges.


The 12-month Amazon Web Services Free Tier period associated with your Amazon Web Services account 195275671096 will expire on March 31, 2026. If no action is taken, your resources will continue to run, and you’ll be automatically billed for any active resources when the 12-month Free Tier period ends.


We strongly advise that you sign in and review your Amazon Web Services Billing & Cost Management Dashboard to locate any active resources on your account that you no longer need. Even if you aren’t using your Amazon Web Services account or have closed the account, it’s possible that you still have active resources.


1. Go to your Billing Dashboard to see the line items by region for each service contributing to your Free Tier usage for the month.
Tip: Select each service or the ‘Expand All’ option to view all active services by region.

2. If you no longer need the resources, terminate them to prevent unwanted charges.

3. Open the Management Console, select the region in the navigation bar where you have any unwanted resources. Enter each service name in the search bar to open its dashboard. Terminate any unwanted resources. Please refer to this guide for detailed steps.
Note: Remember to terminate unwanted resources for each region. Terminating resources in one region will not lead to termination of those resources in other regions.

4. Monitor your Free Tier expiration. Once your short-term trials or 12-month Free Tier period ends, you’ll be charged standard, pay-as-you-go service rates for any active resources.

Sincerely,

Subject: Sales Forecast & Pipeline - March 20th Update

 

Hi team,

 

The following is a summary of Sales Pipeline & Forecast for Q3. Changes from last week's figures are noted. All figures in CAD.

 

 

Notable changes to opportunities in the past week are detailed below. A significant part of the week over week reduction in MRC and increase in NRC is related to the Just Energy win from last week being reclassified from MRC ($45K x 3 Months) to NRC ($136K total).

 

  • Closed Won
    • SCADACore Inc - $25K MRC - New Logo, Hosting
      • Partner deal - referred by Ignite

 

  • Closed Lost
    • BCi Digital - $5K MRC, $7K NRC - New Logo, Hosting
    • National Wildlife Federation - $20K NRC - New Logo, PS

 

  • Newly Created
    • Chorus - $47K MRC, $20K NRC - New Logo, Hosting
    • Cable & Wireless Communications - $6K MRC - Install Base, Hosting

 

  • Close Date Pushed
    • Great-Circle Technologies - $47K MRC, $13K NRC (New Logo, Colocation, pushed from Apr to May)
    • AC Foods - $47K MRC (New Logo, Managed Services, pushed from May to Jun)
    • Latitude.sh - $37K MRC, $43K NRC (New Logo, Colocation, pushed from Mar to Apr)
    • Diversity Travel - $16K MRC, $82K NRC (New Logo, Hyperscale/MS/PS, pushed from Apr to May)
    • Inspirata Canada - $8K (New Logo, Private Cloud, pushed from Apr to May)

 

  • MRC or NRC Changed
    • Just Energy - Closed Won (Install Base, Hosting, 3 month licensing deal)
      • MRC reduced from $45K to $0
      • NRC increased from $0 to $136K

 

***

 

To see performance against targets, opportunity details, or other sales analysis please refer to the Power BI Dashboards (updated daily):

 

Sales Forecasting & Analysis - Power BI Dashboards

 

Thanks,

 

Tom Kelsch

Senior Decision Support Analyst
